feature_notes.mouseSpeedStd: Standard deviation of per-sample mouse speed. Near-zero indicates constant robotic velocity.
feature_notes.mouseAccelerationMean: Mean absolute change in speed between consecutive samples. Near-zero indicates linear bot interpolation.
feature_notes.mouseJerkMean: Mean absolute change in acceleration. Real humans show irregular jerk; bots approach zero.
feature_notes.scrollVelocityStd: Standard deviation of scroll velocity. Bots scroll at constant speed giving near-zero std.
feature_notes.scrollDirectionChanges: Number of times scroll direction reversed. Humans reverse; bots rarely do.
feature_notes.keyIntervalStd: Standard deviation of inter-keystroke intervals. Near-zero indicates programmatic typing.
feature_notes.keyHoldDurationMean: Mean duration (ms) a key is physically held. Programmatic presses produce near-zero hold times.
feature_notes.visibilityChangeRate: Tab visibility changes per minute. Very high rates indicate scripted tab toggling.
feature_notes.meanTimeBetweenVisibilityChanges: Mean ms between visibility change events. Very short gaps are suspicious.
feature_notes.hasWebGL: 1 if WebGL renderer is present, 0 if absent. Headless browsers commonly lack WebGL.
feature_notes.screenViewportRatio: viewport.width / screen.width. Headless environments report exactly 1.0.
feature_notes.devicePixelRatio: Device pixel ratio. Real HiDPI devices report values greater than 1.
feature_notes.touchPoints: navigator.maxTouchPoints. Touch-capable devices are rarely headless automation targets.
feature_notes.isSuspiciousUA: 1 if user-agent contains known headless/automation strings, else 0.
